description:
# Why

`Task.repo` (src/task.rs) is provenance: "the code repo this task
was created in (`origin` URL, else repo path). Set at create."

Two weaknesses in "set at create":

1. Create anchors on *where the ball was filed*, not where its code
   lives. A ball filed from the bare hub, or mirrored in by a
   forge-sync bridge (README "Bridging to an external tracker"),
   gets `repo` = the hub / the bridge — not the code repo. The
   clone that is *definitionally* the code home is the one that runs
   `bl claim`; SKILL.md already says "claim from the clone whose
   code the ball touches."

2. `repo_url::current` falls back to the repo-root *basename* when a
   clone has no `origin` (see tests/delivery_remote.rs — a fresh
   test repo resolves to its dir basename). A create from such a
   clone stamps `repo` with a non-URL basename string nobody can
   fetch.

# Scope

- Stamp / backfill `repo` at `bl claim`: the claiming clone's
  `origin` is the authoritative code home. If `repo` is unset, or
  was create-stamped to a clone that is not the claimer, refine it
  at claim time.
- Guard the `repo_url::current` basename fallback on any auto-write
  path: do not persist a bare basename as `repo` — leave it null
  rather than write a value that cannot be fetched.
- "Freeze" means *implicitly*: after claim, no lifecycle step
  re-stamps `repo` on its own. It is NOT a locked field — an
  explicit `bl update <id> repo=<url>` must still work, for fixups
  when a task is escalated, reassigned, or abandoned and its home
  repo changes. The invariant is "nothing updates it behind your
  back," not "nobody may update it."

# Why not re-stamp on every update

The tempting symmetry — stamp `repo_url::current` on every status
update — is wrong. The clone running the *last* update is typically
the hub or a sibling doing `bl close`: the least authoritative
location. Last-writer-wins makes the field track the closer, not
the code. This is exactly the bl-6816 clobber: `populate_on_close`
recomputes `repo_url::current` and overwrites `delivered_repo` with
the closing clone's URL. Set-once-then-implicitly-frozen is the
deliberate counter to that.

# Origin

Design discussion closing bl-4fbc. Pairs with bl-6816 (same
clobber-class on the sibling field `delivered_repo`).
title: Anchor Task.repo at claim, not create; guard the basename fallback
